Cooper Joseph Sharp

2026-03-26

# ============================================================================
# ICRG: Healthy Control vs. All Gamblers — Paper Analysis
# Author: Cooper Joseph Sharp
# Date:   2026-03-26
# ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(car)
## Warning: package 'car' was built under R version 4.3.3
## Loading required package: carData
## 
## Attaching package: 'car'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     recode
library(broom)
## Warning: package 'broom' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(pscl)
## Classes and Methods for R developed in the
## Political Science Computational Laboratory
## Department of Political Science
## Stanford University
## Simon Jackman
## hurdle and zeroinfl functions by Achim Zeileis
library(lmtest)
## Loading required package: zoo
## 
## Attaching package: 'zoo'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.Date, as.Date.numeric
library(sandwich)
library(tidycensus)
## Warning: package 'tidycensus' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(pROC)
## Warning: package 'pROC' was built under R version 4.3.3
## Type 'citation("pROC")' for a citation.
## 
## Attaching package: 'pROC'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     cov, smooth, var
library(knitr)

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 1. DATA LOADING & PREPARATION
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/2025-ICRG")
df     <- read.csv("derivatives/scoring/analytic-sample.csv")

dat <- df %>%
  filter(pg %in% c(0, 1, 2)) %>%      # Keep everyone, OR just remove this line entirely
  mutate(
    PG     = ifelse(pg == 0, 0L, 1L),  # HC = 0, ANY gambler (1 or 2) = 1
    gender = factor(demo_gender),
    income = factor(income_median_split),
    z_age           = as.numeric(scale(demo_yrs)),
    z_yoe           = as.numeric(scale(ses_yoe)),
    z_fevs          = as.numeric(scale(fevs_total)),
    z_usidep        = as.numeric(scale(usidep_total)),
    z_reward        = as.numeric(scale(rew_composite)),
    z_dospert_inv   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_inv)),
    z_dospert_ethic = as.numeric(scale(dospert_ethic)),
    z_dospert_soc   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_soc)),
    z_dospert_hs    = as.numeric(scale(dospert_hs)),
    z_dospert_rec   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_rec)),
    z_depression    = as.numeric(scale(susd_depression)),
    z_mania         = as.numeric(scale(susd_mania)),
    z_anxiety       = as.numeric(scale(scared_total)),
    z_loneliness    = as.numeric(scale(uclal_total)),
    z_planfulness   = as.numeric(scale(planfulness_mean)),
    z_neigh_disadv  = rowMeans(cbind(
      as.numeric(scale(TotalCrime)),
      as.numeric(scale(unemployed)),
      as.numeric(scale(gini))
    ), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(
    PG, gender, income,
    z_neigh_disadv, z_age, z_yoe,
    z_reward,
    z_dospert_inv, z_dospert_ethic, z_dospert_soc, z_dospert_hs, z_dospert_rec,
    z_depression, z_mania, z_anxiety, z_loneliness, z_planfulness,
    z_fevs, z_usidep
  ) %>%
  filter(gender %in% c(1, 2))

cat("Analytic N =", nrow(dat), "\n")
## Analytic N = 794
cat("HC (PG=0):", sum(dat$PG == 0), "\n")
## HC (PG=0): 378
cat("All Gamblers (PG=1):", sum(dat$PG == 1), "\n")
## All Gamblers (PG=1): 416
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 3. HIERARCHICAL LOGISTIC REGRESSION
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Block 1: Demographics + neighbourhood context
m1 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)

# Block 2: + Reward sensitivity & risk-taking
m2 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)

# Block 3: + Clinical/social symptoms
m3 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)

# Block 4: + Financial vulnerability
m4 <- glm(
  PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe +
    z_reward + z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic +
    z_dospert_inv + z_dospert_rec +
    z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness +
    z_planfulness +
    z_fevs + z_usidep + income,
  data = dat, family = binomial
)


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 4. MODEL COMPARISONS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\n── Likelihood-ratio tests ──\n")
## 
## ── Likelihood-ratio tests ──
anova(m1, m2, test = "Chisq")
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe
## Model 2: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance  Pr(>Chi)    
## 1       786     909.96                          
## 2       780     818.78  6   91.181 < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(m2, m3, test = "Chisq")
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec
## Model 2: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec + z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness + 
##     z_planfulness
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance Pr(>Chi)  
## 1       780     818.78                       
## 2       775     805.32  5   13.463   0.0194 *
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(m3, m4, test = "Chisq")
## Analysis of Deviance Table
## 
## Model 1: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec + z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness + 
##     z_planfulness
## Model 2: PG ~ z_neigh_disadv + gender + z_age + race + z_yoe + z_reward + 
##     z_dospert_hs + z_dospert_soc + z_dospert_ethic + z_dospert_inv + 
##     z_dospert_rec + z_depression + z_mania + z_anxiety + z_loneliness + 
##     z_planfulness + z_fevs + z_usidep + income
##   Resid. Df Resid. Dev Df Deviance Pr(>Chi)  
## 1       775     805.32                       
## 2       772     798.59  3   6.7231  0.08127 .
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
data.frame(
  Model       = c("M1: Context", "M2: + Reward/Risk",
                   "M3: + Clinical", "M4: + Financial"),
  AIC         = round(AIC(m1, m2, m3, m4)$AIC, 1),
  BIC         = round(BIC(m1, m2, m3, m4)$BIC, 1),
  McFadden_R2 = round(c(pR2(m1)["McFadden"], pR2(m2)["McFadden"],
                         pR2(m3)["McFadden"], pR2(m4)["McFadden"]), 3)
) %>% kable()
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
## fitting null model for pseudo-r2
Model AIC BIC McFadden_R2
M1: Context 926.0 963.4 0.172
M2: + Reward/Risk 846.8 912.3 0.255
M3: + Clinical 843.3 932.2 0.267
M4: + Financial 842.6 945.5 0.273 
cat("\n── VIF (M4) ──\n")
## 
## ── VIF (M4) ──
vif(m4)
##                     GVIF Df GVIF^(1/(2*Df))
## z_neigh_disadv  1.077652  1        1.038100
## gender          1.144616  1        1.069867
## z_age           2.018970  1        1.420905
## race            1.222395  3        1.034035
## z_yoe           1.234493  1        1.111078
## z_reward        1.512507  1        1.229840
## z_dospert_hs    3.856811  1        1.963877
## z_dospert_soc   3.734726  1        1.932544
## z_dospert_ethic 5.465916  1        2.337930
## z_dospert_inv   3.557202  1        1.886055
## z_dospert_rec   3.929659  1        1.982337
## z_depression    3.161914  1        1.778177
## z_mania         2.157854  1        1.468964
## z_anxiety       2.904967  1        1.704396
## z_loneliness    1.804060  1        1.343153
## z_planfulness   1.632309  1        1.277619
## z_fevs          1.839031  1        1.356109
## z_usidep        1.497203  1        1.223603
## income          1.191872  1        1.091729
cat("\n── HC3 Robust SEs (M4) ──\n")
## 
## ── HC3 Robust SEs (M4) ──
coeftest(m4, vcov = vcovHC(m4, type = "HC3"))
## 
## z test of coefficients:
## 
##                             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)   
## (Intercept)                -0.211900   0.479295 -0.4421 0.658412   
## z_neigh_disadv              0.379722   0.131663  2.8840 0.003926 **
## gender2                    -0.334680   0.192627 -1.7375 0.082307 . 
## z_age                      -0.204516   0.135922 -1.5047 0.132413   
## raceBlack                   1.482217   0.526424  2.8156 0.004868 **
## raceOther                   0.348763   0.574714  0.6068 0.543953   
## raceWhite                   0.822513   0.475121  1.7312 0.083422 . 
## z_yoe                      -0.028678   0.105821 -0.2710 0.786390   
## z_reward                    0.376823   0.133770  2.8169 0.004848 **
## z_dospert_hs               -0.150139   0.208415 -0.7204 0.471290   
## z_dospert_soc               0.121982   0.227484  0.5362 0.591806   
## z_dospert_ethic             0.091413   0.267098  0.3422 0.732166   
## z_dospert_inv               0.318379   0.208710  1.5255 0.127143   
## z_dospert_rec               0.310703   0.211501  1.4690 0.141823   
## z_depression                0.093612   0.177306  0.5280 0.597520   
## z_mania                     0.202896   0.153054  1.3257 0.184955   
## z_anxiety                  -0.237895   0.167391 -1.4212 0.155262   
## z_loneliness               -0.238657   0.120421 -1.9819 0.047496 * 
## z_planfulness              -0.225189   0.121027 -1.8607 0.062792 . 
## z_fevs                      0.089227   0.128443  0.6947 0.487259   
## z_usidep                    0.143979   0.130154  1.1062 0.268631   
## incomeBelow Median (<$40k) -0.421999   0.198683 -2.1240 0.033672 * 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 5. ODDS RATIOS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

col_pg <- "purple4"

get_or_table <- function(model) {
  est <- coef(model)
  ci  <- suppressMessages(confint(model))
  data.frame(
    term    = names(est),
    OR      = exp(est),
    CI_low  = exp(ci[, 1]),
    CI_high = exp(ci[, 2]),
    row.names = NULL
  )
}

or_m4 <- get_or_table(m4)

tidy(m4, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(across(c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high), ~ round(., 3)),
         p.value = round(p.value, 3),
         sig = case_when(
           p.value < .001 ~ "***",
           p.value < .01  ~ "**",
           p.value < .05  ~ "*",
           p.value < .1   ~ ".",
           TRUE           ~ ""
         )) %>%
  rename(OR = estimate, CI_low = conf.low, CI_high = conf.high) %>%
  kable()
term OR std.error statistic p.value CI_low CI_high sig
(Intercept) 0.809 0.4420120 -0.4793984 0.632 0.333 1.910
z_neigh_disadv 1.462 0.1337758 2.8384976 0.005 1.129 1.908 **
gender2 0.716 0.1880205 -1.7800179 0.075 0.495 1.036 .
z_age 0.815 0.1243831 -1.6442413 0.100 0.639 1.040
raceBlack 4.403 0.4935026 3.0034640 0.003 1.696 11.858 **
raceOther 1.417 0.5441821 0.6408936 0.522 0.491 4.187
raceWhite 2.276 0.4370361 1.8820245 0.060 0.978 5.499 .
z_yoe 0.972 0.1036105 -0.2767819 0.782 0.790 1.188
z_reward 1.458 0.1222879 3.0814421 0.002 1.149 1.858 **
z_dospert_hs 0.861 0.2042250 -0.7351626 0.462 0.575 1.281
z_dospert_soc 1.130 0.2029327 0.6010943 0.548 0.759 1.683
z_dospert_ethic 1.096 0.2456395 0.3721431 0.710 0.677 1.775
z_dospert_inv 1.375 0.1965190 1.6200951 0.105 0.937 2.027
z_dospert_rec 1.364 0.2022669 1.5361046 0.125 0.919 2.034
z_depression 1.098 0.1620654 0.5776212 0.564 0.799 1.510
z_mania 1.225 0.1437952 1.4110056 0.158 0.924 1.625
z_anxiety 0.788 0.1529412 -1.5554657 0.120 0.582 1.061
z_loneliness 0.788 0.1157399 -2.0620100 0.039 0.626 0.986 *
z_planfulness 0.798 0.1117115 -2.0158090 0.044 0.640 0.993 *
z_fevs 1.093 0.1164221 0.7664051 0.443 0.870 1.374
z_usidep 1.155 0.1267768 1.1356903 0.256 0.902 1.484
incomeBelow Median (<$40k) 0.656 0.1922918 -2.1945745 0.028 0.449 0.956 * 
or_m4 %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(term = reorder(term, OR)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(OR, term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = CI_low, xmax = CI_high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = col_pg) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(x = "Odds Ratio (log scale)", y = NULL,
       title = "Odds Ratios — HC vs. All Gamblers (M4)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 6. ROC / AUC
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

col_ng <- "pink3"
col_rg <- "purple1"

roc1 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m1, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)
roc2 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m2, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)
roc3 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m3, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)
roc4 <- roc(dat$PG, predict(m4, type = "response"), quiet = TRUE)

auc_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c("M1: Context", "M2: + Reward/Risk",
            "M3: + Clinical", "M4: + Financial"),
  AUC   = round(c(auc(roc1), auc(roc2), auc(roc3), auc(roc4)), 3)
)
kable(auc_table)
Model AUC
M1: Context 0.769
M2: + Reward/Risk 0.816
M3: + Clinical 0.825
M4: + Financial 0.827 
cat("\n── DeLong Tests ──\n")
## 
## ── DeLong Tests ──
cat("M1 vs M2:\n"); print(roc.test(roc1, roc2, method = "delong"))
## M1 vs M2:
## 
##  DeLong's test for two correlated ROC curves
## 
## data:  roc1 and roc2
## Z = -4.5993, p-value = 4.24e-06
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in AUC is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.06745829 -0.02714387
## sample estimates:
## AUC of roc1 AUC of roc2 
##   0.7687411   0.8160422
cat("M2 vs M3:\n"); print(roc.test(roc2, roc3, method = "delong"))
## M2 vs M3:
## 
##  DeLong's test for two correlated ROC curves
## 
## data:  roc2 and roc3
## Z = -2.0804, p-value = 0.03749
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in AUC is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.0166488026 -0.0004960895
## sample estimates:
## AUC of roc1 AUC of roc2 
##   0.8160422   0.8246146
cat("M3 vs M4:\n"); print(roc.test(roc3, roc4, method = "delong"))
## M3 vs M4:
## 
##  DeLong's test for two correlated ROC curves
## 
## data:  roc3 and roc4
## Z = -0.84994, p-value = 0.3954
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in AUC is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.008052228  0.003180942
## sample estimates:
## AUC of roc1 AUC of roc2 
##   0.8246146   0.8270503
ggroc(
  list(
    "M1: Context"       = roc1,
    "M2: + Reward/Risk" = roc2,
    "M3: + Clinical"    = roc3,
    "M4: + Financial"   = roc4
  ),
  linewidth = 1
) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 1, slope = 1,
              linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("M1: Context"       = "grey70",
               "M2: + Reward/Risk" = col_ng,
               "M3: + Clinical"    = col_rg,
               "M4: + Financial"   = col_pg)
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.25, y = 0.30, hjust = 0, size = 3.8,
           label = paste0(
             "AUC M1 = ", auc_table$AUC[1], "\n",
             "AUC M2 = ", auc_table$AUC[2], "\n",
             "AUC M3 = ", auc_table$AUC[3], "\n",
             "AUC M4 = ", auc_table$AUC[4]
           )) +
  labs(x = "Specificity", y = "Sensitivity", colour = "Model",
       title = "ROC Curves: HC vs. All Gamblers") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

# ── Classification at Youden's J ────────────────────────────────────────────
best_thresh <- coords(roc4, "best",
                      ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity",
                              "specificity", "ppv", "npv"))
cat("\n── Optimal Cut-Point (Youden's J) ──\n")
## 
## ── Optimal Cut-Point (Youden's J) ──
print(best_thresh)
##           threshold sensitivity specificity       ppv       npv
## threshold 0.6313921   0.6346154   0.8730159 0.8461538 0.6846473
predicted_class <- ifelse(predict(m4, type = "response") > best_thresh$threshold, 1, 0)
cat("\n── Confusion Matrix ──\n")
## 
## ── Confusion Matrix ──
table(Predicted = predicted_class, Actual = dat$PG)
##          Actual
## Predicted   0   1
##         0 330 152
##         1  48 264
cat("Accuracy:", round(mean(predicted_class == dat$PG, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")
## Accuracy: 0.748
